fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Wyoming Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Wyoming - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity wyoming, how to transfer dietitian license to wyoming, dietitian compact wyoming, wyoming dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Wyoming Dietetics Licensing Board
board.mailing_address: Wyoming Board of Dietetics, 122 West 25th Street, Suite 106, Herschler Building, Cheyenne, WY 82002

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: The credential required to practice dietetics in Wyoming. Wyoming is a 'Practice Act and Title Protection state', meaning you must hold this license to provide dietetic services.

steps.title: Create an Account on the Wyoming Professional Licensing Portal
steps.description: Navigate to the Wyoming Professional Licensing portal, select 'Dietetics', and register for a new user account if you do not have one already.

steps.title: Start a New Dietetics License Application
steps.description: Log in to your account on the Wyoming Professional Licensing portal and initiate a new application for a Dietetics license. Be sure to indicate if you are applying by endorsement from another state.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the application, providing accurate personal, educational, and professional background details.

steps.title: Upload Required Documents
steps.description: Submit electronic copies of official transcripts verifying a bachelor's or master's degree, proof of supervised practice (e.g., dietetic internship verification), and verification of current registration as a Registered Dietitian (RD) with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable initial application fee of $250.00 through the Wyoming Professional Licensing portal.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor Your Application
steps.description: Electronically submit your completed application. The Board reviews applications at its regularly scheduled meetings, so monitor the Wyoming Professional Licensing portal for status updates or requests for additional information.

documents.detail: Application accurately filled out along with proof of U.S. citizenship or lawful presence.
Proof of active registration sent directly from the CDR.

provisional_text: Wyoming regulations do not feature a standard provisional or temporary state license. You must complete the CDR examination before applying.
reciprocity_text: Wyoming offers licensure by endorsement. Applicants holding a valid and unrestricted license in another state with equivalent requirements, or holding active CDR registration, can apply using the standard application method by demonstrating out-of-state verification.

faqs.question: How much does a Wyoming dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $200.

faqs.question: When does the Wyoming license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Wyoming dietitian licenses expire biennially. The renewal fee is $200.

faqs.question: Is licensure required to practice in Wyoming?
faqs.answer: Yes, Wyoming has a Practice Act that legally mandates obtaining a state license to practice dietetics and to use the protected title of Dietitian.

faqs.question: Does Wyoming have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Wyoming has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Wyoming?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 1 to 2 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Wyoming while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Wyoming offers licensure by endorsement. Applicants holding a valid and unrestricted license in another state with equivalent requirements, or holding active CDR registration, can apply using the standard application method by demonstrating out-of-state verification.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Wyoming
hero.dek: To become a Licensed Dietitian (LD) in Wyoming, applicants must apply through the Wyoming Professional Licensing online portal.
